segments.text: Why does the Almighty not reserve times for judgment? Why may those who know Him never see His days?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Men move boundary stones; they pasture stolen flocks.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They drive away the donkey of the fatherless and take the widow’s ox in pledge.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They push the needy off the road and force all the poor of the land into hiding.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Indeed, like wild donkeys in the desert, the poor go to work foraging for food;
the wasteland is food for their children.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They gather fodder in the fields and glean the vineyards of the wicked.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Without clothing, they spend the night naked; they have no covering against the cold.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Drenched by mountain rains, they huddle against the rocks for want of shelter.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The fatherless infant is snatched from the breast; the nursing child of the poor is seized for a debt.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Without clothing, they wander about naked. They carry the sheaves, but still go hungry.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They crush olives within their walls; they tread the winepresses, but go thirsty.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
From the city, men groan, and the souls of the wounded cry out,
yet God charges no one with wrongdoing.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then there are those who rebel against the light, not knowing its ways or staying on its paths.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
When daylight is gone, the murderer risesto kill the poor and needy;
in the night he is like a thief.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The eye of the adulterer watches for twilight. Thinking, ‘No eye will see me,’ he covers his face.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves in,
never to experience the light.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For to them, deep darkness is their morning; surely they are friends with the terrors of darkness!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
They are but foam on the surface of the water; their portion of the land is cursed,
so that no one turns toward their vineyards.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: As drought and heat consume the melting snow, so Sheol steals those who have sinned.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The womb forgets them; the worm feeds on them; they are remembered no more. So injustice is like a broken tree.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They prey on the barren and childless, and show no kindness to the widow.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Yet by His power, God drags away the mighty; though rising up, they have no assurance of life.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He gives them a sense of security, but His eyes are on their ways.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They are exalted for a moment, then they are gone; they are brought low and gathered up like all others; they are cut off like heads of grain.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If this is not so, then who can prove me a liar and reduce my words to nothing?
segments.speaker: Unknown
